title: The Magicians of Caprona — Chapter 2 (Fragment 2)
sourceText:
The Piazza Nuova had been made at the same time as the New Bridge, about seventy years ago, to give everyone the grand view of Caprona Tonino was looking at now. It was breathtaking. But the trouble was, everything Tonino looked at had something to do with the Casa Montana.

Take the Ducal Palace, whose golden-stone towers cut clear lines into the clean blue of the sky opposite. Each golden tower swept outwards at the top, so that the soldiers on the battlements, beneath the snapping red and gold flags, could not be reached by anyone climbing up from below. Tonino could see the shields built into the battlements, two a side, one cherry, one leaf-green, showing that the Montanas and the Petrocchis had added a spell to defend each tower. And the great white marble front below was inlaid with other marbles, all colors of the rainbow. And among those colors were cherry-red and leaf-green.

The long golden villas on the hillside below the Palace each had a leaf-green or cherry-red disc on their walls. Some were half hidden by the dark spires of the elegant little trees planted in front of them, but Tonino knew they were there. And the stone and metal arches of the New Bridge, sweeping away from him towards the villas and the Palace, each bore an enamel plaque, green and red alternately. The New Bridge had been sustained by the strongest spells the Casa Montana and the Casa Petrocchi could produce. At the moment, when the river was just a shingly trickle, they did not seem necessary. But in winter, when the rain fell in the Apennines, the Voltava became a furious torrent. The arches of the New Bridge barely cleared it. The Old Bridge—which Tonino could see by craning out and sideways—was often under water, and the funny little houses along it could not be used. Only Montana and Petrocchi spells deep in its foundations stopped the Old Bridge being swept away.

Tonino had heard Old Niccolo say that the New Bridge spells had taken the entire efforts of the entire Montana family. Old Niccolo had helped make them when he was the same age as Tonino. Tonino could not have done. Miserable, he looked down at the golden walls and red pan-tiles of Caprona below. He was quite certain that every single one hid at least a leaf-green scrip. And the most Tonino had ever done was help stamp the winged horse on the outside. He was fairly sure that was all he ever would do.

He had a feeling somebody was calling him. Tonino looked round at the Piazza Nuova. No body. Despite the view, the Piazza was too far for the tourists to come. All Tonino could see were the mighty iron griffins which reared up at intervals all around the parapet, reaching iron paws to the sky. More griffins tangled into a fighting heap in the center of the square to make a fountain. And even here, Tonino could not get away from his family. A little metal plate was set into the stone beneath the huge iron claws of the nearest griffin. It was leaf-green. Tonino found he had burst into tears.

Among his tears, he thought for a moment that one of the more distant griffins had left its stone perch and come trotting round the parapet towards him. It had left its wings behind, or else had them tightly folded.

He was told, a little smugly, that cats do not need wings. Benvenuto sat down on the parapet beside him, staring accusingly.

Tonino had always been thoroughly in awe of Benvenuto. He stretched out a hand to him timidly. “Hallo, Benvenuto.”

words.definition: Extremely impressive or beautiful.
words.example: It was breathtaking.
words.exampleText: It was breathtaking.

words.definition: Relating to a duke or dukedom.
words.example: Take the Ducal Palace, whose golden-stone towers cut clear lines into the clean blue of the sky opposite.
words.exampleText: Take the Ducal Palace, whose golden-stone towers cut clear lines into the clean blue of the sky opposite.

words.definition: A parapet at the top of a wall, especially of a fort or castle, with gaps for shooting through.
words.example: The soldiers on the battlements, beneath the snapping red and gold flags, could not be reached by anyone climbing up from below.
words.exampleText: The soldiers on the battlements, beneath the snapping red and gold flags, could not be reached by anyone climbing up from below.

words.definition: Set into the surface of something as a decoration.
words.example: The great white marble front below was inlaid with other marbles, all colors of the rainbow.
words.exampleText: The great white marble front below was inlaid with other marbles, all colors of the rainbow.

words.definition: A smooth, hard coating on metal, often decorative.
words.example: The stone and metal arches of the New Bridge, sweeping away from him towards the villas and the Palace, each bore an enamel plaque.
words.exampleText: The stone and metal arches of the New Bridge, sweeping away from him towards the villas and the Palace, each bore an enamel plaque.

words.definition: A strong and fast-moving stream of water or other liquid.
words.example: The Voltava became a furious torrent.
words.exampleText: The Voltava became a furious torrent.

words.definition: Covered with or consisting of small pebbles or shingle.
words.example: When the river was just a shingly trickle, they did not seem necessary.
words.exampleText: When the river was just a shingly trickle, they did not seem necessary.

words.definition: Stretching the neck in order to see something better.
words.example: Tonino could see by craning out and sideways.
words.exampleText: Tonino could see by craning out and sideways.

words.definition: Kept going over a period of time; maintained.
words.example: The New Bridge had been sustained by the strongest spells the Casa Montana and the Casa Petrocchi could produce.
words.exampleText: The New Bridge had been sustained by the strongest spells the Casa Montana and the Casa Petrocchi could produce.

words.definition: The solid base or groundwork of a building or other structure.
words.example: Only Montana and Petrocchi spells deep in its foundations stopped the Old Bridge being swept away.
words.exampleText: Only Montana and Petrocchi spells deep in its foundations stopped the Old Bridge being swept away.

words.definition: Very unhappy or uncomfortable.
words.example: Miserable, he looked down at the golden walls.
words.exampleText: Miserable, he looked down at the golden walls.

words.definition: A low protective wall along the edge of a roof, bridge, or balcony.
words.example: The mighty iron griffins which reared up at intervals all around the parapet.
words.exampleText: The mighty iron griffins which reared up at intervals all around the parapet.

words.definition: A mythical creature with the body of a lion and the head and wings of an eagle.
words.example: Mighty iron griffins which reared up at intervals all around the parapet.
words.exampleText: Mighty iron griffins which reared up at intervals all around the parapet.

words.definition: Rose up on hind legs or lifting upward.
words.example: Griffins which reared up at intervals.
words.exampleText: Griffins which reared up at intervals.

words.definition: Moving at a moderate pace faster than a walk.
words.example: One of the more distant griffins had come trotting round the parapet.
words.exampleText: One of the more distant griffins had come trotting round the parapet.

words.definition: In a self-satisfied or conceited manner.
words.example: He was told, a little smugly, that cats do not need wings.
words.exampleText: He was told, a little smugly, that cats do not need wings.

words.definition: In a way that suggests blaming or faulting someone.
words.example: Benvenuto sat down on the parapet beside him, staring accusingly.
words.exampleText: Benvenuto sat down on the parapet beside him, staring accusingly.

words.definition: Tastefully fine or luxurious in dress, style, or design.
words.example: Dark spires of the elegant little trees planted in front of them.
words.exampleText: Dark spires of the elegant little trees planted in front of them.

words.definition: A pleasing view, especially one seen through a long, narrow opening.
words.example: To give everyone the grand view of Caprona Tonino was looking at now.
words.exampleText: To give everyone the grand view of Caprona Tonino was looking at now.

words.definition: Going upward using hands and feet.
words.example: Soldiers on the battlements could not be reached by anyone climbing up from below.
words.exampleText: Soldiers on the battlements could not be reached by anyone climbing up from below.

words.definition: To make a sudden sharp cracking sound.
words.example: Beneath the snapping red and gold flags.
words.exampleText: Beneath the snapping red and gold flags.

words.definition: A form of words or actions believed to have magical power.
words.example: The Montanas and the Petrocchis had added a spell to defend each tower.
words.exampleText: The Montanas and the Petrocchis had added a spell to defend each tower.

words.definition: A descriptive colour term referring to bright green, like fresh leaves.
words.example: Cherry-red and leaf-green discs on the villas' walls.
words.exampleText: Cherry-red and leaf-green discs on the villas' walls.

words.definition: A vivid red colour resembling that of cherries.
words.example: Cherry-red flags and discs appeared on the buildings.
words.exampleText: Cherry-red flags and discs appeared on the buildings.

words.definition: To impress a mark onto something.
words.example: Help stamp the winged horse on the outside.
words.exampleText: Help stamp the winged horse on the outside.
